title: App Stylesheet (App.css)
content:
## App Stylesheet (App.css)

This file contains the global CSS styles for the DocMed application. It defines the color palette, typography, layout structure, and styling for various components including the sidebar, search bar, navigation, main content area, and markdown rendering.

### Purpose

- **Theming**: Establishes a consistent visual theme with defined CSS variables for colors and spacing.
- **Layout**: Defines the core flexbox layout for the application, separating the sidebar and main content.
- **Component Styling**: Provides specific styles for UI elements like buttons, inputs, navigation items, cards, and code blocks.
- **Responsiveness**: Includes styles that adapt to different screen sizes, particularly for the sidebar (collapsed/expanded states) and grid layouts.
- **Markdown Rendering**: Styles the output of markdown content to ensure readability and consistency.

### Key Styles and Variables

**CSS Variables (Root Scope):**

- `--bg`: Background color.
- `--surface`: Primary surface color (e.g., for cards, sidebar).
- `--surface-2`: Secondary surface color.
- `--border`: Primary border color.
- `--border-2`: Secondary border color.
- `--text`: Primary text color.
- `--text-2`: Secondary text color.
- `--text-3`: Tertiary text color.
- `--accent`: Accent color for interactive elements and highlights.
- `--accent-dim`: Dimmed accent color.
- `--sidebar-w`: Width of the sidebar.
- `--radius`: Border radius for elements.

**Layout Classes:**

- `.layout`: Main flex container for sidebar and content.
- `.sidebar`: Styles for the collapsible sidebar.
- `.sidebar.collapsed`: Styles when the sidebar is collapsed.
- `.main`: Styles for the main content area.

**Component Classes:**

- `.repo-title`: Repository title in the sidebar header.
- `.collapse-btn`: Button to collapse/expand the sidebar.
- `.search-wrap`, `.search-icon`, `.search-input`, `.search-clear`: Styles for the search functionality.
- `.nav`: Navigation container.
- `.nav-item`: Individual navigation link/item.
- `.nav-badge`: Badge for navigation items.
- `.nav-section-label`: Label for navigation sections.
- `.nav-dir`, `.nav-file`: Styles for directory and file navigation entries.
- `.page`: Container for page content.
- `.page-title`, `.page-subtitle`: Heading styles for pages.
- `.stat-card`, `.stat-value`, `.stat-label`: Styles for statistics cards.
- `.file-grid`, `.file-card`, `.file-card-name`, `.file-card-path`, `.file-card-title`: Styles for file display grids.
- `.commit-list`, `.commit-item`, `.commit-sha`, `.commit-msg`, `.commit-meta`: Styles for commit list items.
- `.doc-filepath`: File path display in documentation pages.
- `.markdown-body`: Styles for rendered markdown content (headings, paragraphs, links, code, tables, etc.).
- `.empty-state`: Styling for when there's no content to display.
- `.loading-pulse`: Animation for loading states.

### Usage

This stylesheet is typically imported directly into the main application component (e.g., `App.js` or `main.jsx`) to apply global styles. Developers can reference these classes in their component JSX to style elements according to the defined theme and layout.

**Example:**

To style a button as a primary action using the accent color:

```jsx
<button className="accent-button">Click Me</button>
```

*(Note: While `accent-button` is not explicitly defined in the provided CSS, this illustrates how a developer might create a new class that leverages the defined `--accent` variable for styling.)*

Developers can also extend existing styles by adding new classes or by modifying these global styles with caution, being mindful of the overall application theme.
